course_description:
Advances student's clinical practice technical skills of assessment and treatment for sports physiotherapists.

This course builds on the advanced knowledge and skills developed in PHTY7931, PHTY7932, PHTY7933 and PHTY7934 to develop a sports physiotherapist's ability to be effective and reflect on prevention and management of physical activity and sports related injuries. This course emphasizes the integration of clinical skills and knowledge within the management of specific injuries/disorders in individuals with a sport and/or exercise focus.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

timetable:
Additional timetable information
Full timetable information available on Blackboard.
course_aims: To develop advanced clinical reasoning and practical skills in the management and prevention of sports injuries and musculoskeletal disorders.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate advanced level skills of assessment and treatment of the articular, muscular and neural system.
Demonstrate an understanding of risk management principles and ethical considerations in the safe and effective management of clients with sports injuries and musculoskeletal conditions.
Understand and explain the rationale for the selection of assessment and treatment techniques/plans for sports injuries and musculoskeletal conditions.
Understand and explain the evidence underpinning physiotherapy management to the client and the multi-discipinary health/sports team.
Review and synthesis the literature to understand and communicate underlying pathology, risk factors and management of common injuries among individuals who participate in sport and exercise.

assessment_details.title: Objective Structured Clinical Exam #1
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This exam will assess practical physiotherapy skills in a practical station exam. It will cover content delivered between March 2nd and March 12th. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by other means such as audio recording or detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date. assessment_details.title: In-Semester Written Exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This exam will assess students understanding of course content in a case-based online written exam. This assessment task is to be completed online. Inspera Assessment may be used for this assessment. Further details, including an introduction to Inspera functionality, practice opportunities, and where to go for support, will be provided via Blackboard during semester. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Sports Analysis Presentation
assessment_details.task_description: Students will select a sport and deliver an individual oral presentation that presents their analysis of a fundamental skill or movement which is a key component of the sport (i.e., a tennis serve, volleyball spike, rugby tackle, the eggbeater kick in water polo, etc…), and discusses the management of a specific injury typically associated with the task. The oral presentations will be delivered online. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by other means such as audio recording or detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extensions can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for an extension request and provide evidence of circumstance as soon as it becomes evident that an extension is needed, but no later than the assessment item submission date. Further information regarding what constitutes an acceptable reason for an extension and how to apply for an assessment extensions can be found at: Applying for an extension - my.UQ - University of Queensland .

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 3 (23 Feb - 15 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Advanced Sports and Exercise Physiotherapy (Lectures and clinical discussion) Students will listen to lectures and participate in interactive discussions on a range of topics of relevance to sports and exercise physiotherapy. These learning activities are undertaken in person on campus during weeks 2 and 3, and independently online is week 1. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 11 (23 Feb - 17 May)
learning_activities.topic: Advanced Musculoskeletal and Sports Physiotherapy (Independent optional preparation) Students have the option of reviewing and learning practical musculoskeletal and sports physiotherapy skills through online video instruction for preparation for their Advanced Musculoskeletal and Sports Physiotherapy Clinical Practice Intensive. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 13 (02 Mar - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Advanced Musculoskeletal and Sports Physiotherapy (Clinical practical series) Students will learn practical musculoskeletal, sports and exercise physiotherapy skills through in-class demonstration, feedback on handling and exercise prescription, and application to clinical cases. These learning activities are undertaken in person on campus throughout weeks 2, 3, 12 and 13 of Semester. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 10 (09 Mar - 10 May)
learning_activities.topic: Sports Analysis Presentation Feedback Session Staff will provide feedback on the planning and development of your sports analysis presentation during an online feedback session. Students should come to the session with any specific questions they have about their sports analysis presentation, and be prepared to discussion their progress to date. This session will not be recorded. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
